perturbation.original_text: No later than five (5) business days prior to each annual or special meeting of stockholders held prior to the expiration of the Standstill Period, each Investor shall, and shall cause each of its Associates and Affiliates to, vote any shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by such Investors in accordance with this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: No later than two (2) business days prior to each annual or special meeting of stockholders held prior to the expiration of the Standstill Period, each Investor shall, and shall cause each of its Associates and Affiliates to, vote any shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by such Investors in accordance with this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the deadline to vote shares of Common Stock could violate proxy rules that are reasonably calculated and provide sufficient time for proper notice and voting.
perturbation.law_explanation: SEC Rule 14a-16 mandates timely distribution of proxy materials. Reducing the voting deadline to two business days before a meeting may impede shareholders' ability to review materials and vote, potentially violating the rule's intent to facilitate informed voting.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.14a-16 - Internet availability of proxy materials. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.14a-16 - Internet availability of proxy materials. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.14a-16 Internet availability of proxy materials. (a) (1) A registrant shall furnish a proxy statement pursuant to § 240.14a-3(a) , or an annual report to security holders pursuant to § 240.14a-3(b) , to a security holder by sending the security holder a Notice of Internet Availability of Proxy Materials, as described in this section , 40 calendar days or more prior to the security holder meeting date, or if no meeting is to be held, 40 calendar days or more prior to the date the votes, consents or authorizations may be used to effect the corporate action , and complying with all other requirements of this section . (2) Unless the registrant chooses to follow the full set delivery option set forth in paragraph (n) of this section , it must provide the record holder or respondent bank with all information listed in paragraph (d) of this section in sufficient time for the record holder or respondent bank to prepare, print and send a Notice of Internet Availability of Proxy Materials to beneficial owners at least 40 calendar days before the meeting date. (b) (1) All materials identified in the Notice of Internet Availability of Proxy Materials must be publicly accessible, free of charge, at the Web site address specified in the notice on or before the time that the notice is sent to the security holder and such materials must remain available on that Web site through the conclusion of the meeting of security holders. (2) All additional soliciting materials sent to security holders or made public after the Notice of Internet Availability of Proxy Materials has been sent must be made publicly accessible at the specified Web site address no later than the day on which such materials
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time frame for investors to vote shares from five to two business days. The Law Explanation suggests that this reduction may impede shareholders' ability to review materials and vote, potentially violating the intent of SEC rules. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing 17 CFR § 240.14a-16, states that registrants must furnish proxy statements at least 40 calendar days prior to the security holder meeting date. While the snippet focuses on the *furnishing* of materials rather than the voting deadline, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both argue that reducing the voting deadline to two days before the meeting creates a contradiction because it is insufficient time. The original text's five-day period, while not explicitly mandated by the snippet, likely allows for compliance with proxy rules, whereas the changed text's two-day window creates a legally questionable situation. Since the inconsistency in deadlines creates a situation where compliance is doubtful, and the Perturbation Type is specifically Inconsistencies - Legal Contradiction, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following the execution of this Agreement, the Company and the Investor Group shall issue a mutually agreeable press release (the "Mutual Press Release"), announcing certain terms of this Agreement, substantially in the form attached hereto as Exhibit C.
perturbation.changed_text: Ten (10) business days following the execution of this Agreement, the Company and the Investor Group shall issue a mutually agreeable press release (the "Mutual Press Release"), announcing certain terms of this Agreement, substantially in the form attached hereto as Exhibit C.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying the press release announcing the cooperation agreement could run afoul of securities laws requiring timely disclosure of material information.
perturbation.law_explanation: SEC Rule 10b-5 prohibits omissions of material facts that would make statements misleading. Delaying the press release for ten business days could be construed as withholding material information from the public, especially if the agreement impacts the company's operations or stock price.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a ten-day delay in issuing a press release, which contradicts the requirement for timely disclosure of material information under securities laws. SEC Rule 10b-5, as indicated in both Scraped Law Snippet 1 and Scraped Law Snippet 2, prohibits omissions of material facts that would make statements misleading. Delaying the press release could be seen as withholding material information, potentially violating this rule. The original text mandated prompt action, while the modified text allows a delay, creating an inconsistency with the need for timely disclosure, thus creating a contradiction as defined under 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction'.
